name: Brussels

overview.description: Brussels is Belgium's capital and the administrative heart of the European Union. It's a bilingual (French/Dutch), multicultural city with a large Indian community, easy rail links to the rest of Europe, and a compact historic centre around the Grand Place.
whyVisit: UNESCO-listed Grand Place, one of Europe's most beautiful squares
EU Quarter and major European institutions
Home to the largest Indian community and Embassy of India in Belgium
Excellent rail hub — trains to Paris, Amsterdam, Cologne and London

attractions.name: Grand Place / Grote Markt
attractions.description: UNESCO World Heritage central square surrounded by guildhalls and the Town Hall.

attractions.name: Atomium
attractions.description: Iconic 1958 World's Fair structure with an observation deck and exhibitions.

attractions.name: Manneken Pis
attractions.description: Brussels' famous small bronze fountain statue.

attractions.name: Royal Palace of Brussels
attractions.description: Official palace of the King of the Belgians, open to visitors in summer.

attractions.name: Parc du Cinquantenaire
attractions.description: Grand park with triumphal arch and museums, popular for walks and picnics.

transportation.description: Dense network covering the whole city; the fastest way to reach the EU Quarter and outer neighbourhoods.
tips: Buy a Brupass day pass if making 3+ trips
Metro runs until midnight, later on weekends with Noctis night buses

transportation.description: Three main stations — Brussels-Midi (Eurostar/Thalys), Brussels-Central, and Brussels-Nord.
tips: Midi is best for international trains
Central is closest to the Grand Place

hotels.name: City Centre / Grand Place area
hotels.notes: Walkable to major sights, can be noisy on weekends.

hotels.name: EU Quarter / Schuman
hotels.notes: Business-friendly, quieter evenings, close to Embassy of India.

foodSpots.name: Indian restaurants around Ixelles & Saint-Josse
foodSpots.notes: Highest concentration of Indian restaurants and grocery stores in Belgium.

tips: Brussels is bilingual — street signs appear in both French and Dutch.
Validate public transport tickets before boarding to avoid fines.
Sunday mornings often mean quieter streets but some shops closed.
